issue.message: Use of LayoutInflater.from(requireContext()) detected. Consider using getLayoutInflater() instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
issue.errorLine1: return String.format("In progress at %d:%02d", posSec / 60, posSec % 60);

issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `toLowerCase(Locale)` instead. For strings meant to be internal use `Locale.ROOT`, otherwise `Locale.getDefault()`.
Field requires API level 26 (current min is 23): `android.os.VibrationEffect#DEFAULT_AMPLITUDE`
Call requires API level 24, or core library desugaring (current min is 23): `java.util.List#sort`

This intent filter has the format of an Android App Link but is missing the `autoVerify` attribute; add `android:autoVerify="true"` to ensure your domain will be validated and enable App Link-related Lint warnings. If you do not want clicked URLs to bring the user to your app, remove the `android.intent.category.BROWSABLE` category, or set `android:autoVerify="false"` to make it clear this is not intended to be an Android App Link.

This intent filter has the format of an Android App Link but is missing the `autoVerify` attribute; add `android:autoVerify="true"` to ensure your domain will be validated and enable App Link-related Lint warnings. If you do not want clicked URLs to bring the user to your app, remove the `android.intent.category.BROWSABLE` category, or set `android:autoVerify="false"` to make it clear this is not intended to be an Android App Link.

This intent filter has the format of an Android App Link but is missing the `autoVerify` attribute; add `android:autoVerify="true"` to ensure your domain will be validated and enable App Link-related Lint warnings. If you do not want clicked URLs to bring the user to your app, remove the `android.intent.category.BROWSABLE` category, or set `android:autoVerify="false"` to make it clear this is not intended to be an Android App Link.

This intent filter has the format of an Android App Link but is missing the `autoVerify` attribute; add `android:autoVerify="true"` to ensure your domain will be validated and enable App Link-related Lint warnings. If you do not want clicked URLs to bring the user to your app, remove the `android.intent.category.BROWSABLE` category, or set `android:autoVerify="false"` to make it clear this is not intended to be an Android App Link.

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).

Use of this function is discouraged because resource reflection makes it harder to perform build optimizations and compile-time verification of code. It is much more efficient to retrieve resources by identifier (e.g. `R.foo.bar`) than by name (e.g. `getIdentifier("bar", "foo", null)`).
Must use `app:tint` instead of `android:tint`
Using `setJavaScriptEnabled` can introduce XSS vulnerabilities into your application, review carefully

The following images appear both as density independent `.xml` files and as bitmap files: src\main\res\drawable\ic_launcher_background.xml, src\main\res\mipmap-hdpi\ic_launcher_background.png
The following images appear both as density independent `.xml` files and as bitmap files: src\main\res\drawable\ic_launcher_foreground.xml, src\main\res\mipmap-hdpi\ic_launcher_foreground.png
The following images appear both as density independent `.xml` files and as bitmap files: src\main\res\drawable\ic_launcher_monochrome.xml, src\main\res\mipmap-xxxhdpi\ic_launcher_monochrome.png
The following images appear both as density independent `.xml` files and as bitmap files: src\main\res\drawable-anydpi\ic_notification.xml, src\main\res\drawable-hdpi\ic_notification.png
The following images appear both as density independent `.xml` files and as bitmap files: src\main\res\drawable-anydpi\ic_notification_new.xml, src\main\res\drawable-hdpi\ic_notification_new.png

Buttons in button bars should be borderless; use `style="?android:attr/buttonBarButtonStyle"` (and `?android:attr/buttonBarStyle` on the parent)
